invalid_review_attempts.reason: Malformed non-verdict response; the result text was not the required JSON review object and is not counted as a valid pack-level Claude review.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-165 is a clean descriptor-only continuation of the RP04 master-data closeout series. The contract bumps to v0.10 with current_pack/upstream/next bindings, range RP04.P05.M06.S04-RP04.P06.M04.S17, and a CP00-166 handoff that are internally consistent; CP164 ends at S03 and CP165 starts at S04 with no gap. Coverage distributions sum exactly to 150 (deliverable 28+7+59+24+15+3+14; phases 69+81; micro-phases 17/20/20/8/4/11/11/20/22/17), and the permission-matrix workflow, decision descriptors, catalog, and validators construct only frozen plain objects. No-write and permission/audit boundaries hold: every result sets evaluates_runtime_permission, appends_audit_event, dispatches_review/approval_route, executes_api_handler, renders_ui, mutates_dom, executes_ai_retrieval/analytics to false, matched rules are captured as references only (exposes_raw_rule false), and audit events are expectation-only with no append. All data is synthetic (no real client data), so there is no data leakage. Hermes (H04) and Claude (C04, opus-4-8/max/read_only) evidence packets and the closeout handoff are well-formed and validator-gated. Two non-blocking P3 advisories: internal reference IDs appear on the named customer-facing surface (no runtime leak, but worth confirming before downstream rendering), and two new false-valued route-dispatch attestation keys are required by the validator loop but not visible as additions in the contract diff (operator-reported validation passing implies presence). Approved.

boundary_assessment.notes: CP00-165 builds frozen permission matrix workflow descriptors, decision descriptors, and synthetic fixture-tail references only; package code does not execute runtime permission checks, audit appends, API handlers, UI rendering, AI retrieval, analytics, Hermes, or Claude.

findings.title: customer_facing_decision embeds internal reference identifiers on the renderable surface
findings.description: In createMasterDataPermissionMatrixDecisionDescriptor the object explicitly named customer_facing_decision includes permission_ref, audit_hint_ref, and matched_rule_ref. leak_guard.renderable_surface is declared 'customer_facing_permission_matrix_descriptors_only', yet leak_guard.prohibited_outputs only screens for *_payload / raw_* / *_internal_rule style names, so the substring scan in prohibited_output_absent passes these reference identifiers. The values here are synthetic descriptor refs (e.g. 'matched_rule_cp165_denied_export', 'audit_hint_cp165'), so there is no actual data leak and no runtime rendering occurs in this descriptor-only pack, but a strict customer-facing permission response would typically omit internal rule/audit reference IDs.
findings.recommendation: Before any downstream RP04.P06 pack renders this surface, confirm whether permission_ref/audit_hint_ref/matched_rule_ref belong on customer_facing_decision or only on the internal evidence layer (matched_rule_capture/audit_hint_fields), and if they must not be customer-visible, extend leak_guard to screen reference-style field names rather than only *_payload/raw_* substrings.
findings.post_review_disposition: deferred: CP00-165 has no rendering or runtime exposure; downstream RP04.P06 rendering or real-data packs must decide whether customer_facing_decision may include reference-style fields and harden leak guards before rendering.

findings.title: New false-valued attestation keys required by the validator loop are not visible in the contract diff
findings.description: The validator loop in scripts/validate-rp04-master-data-contract.mjs now iterates Object.entries(MASTER_DATA_CP165_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION) and asserts each key exists in contract.no_write_attestation. CP165_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (registry.js) spreads CP164 and additionally sets several false-valued keys including dispatches_review_route and dispatches_approval_route, which are new permission-matrix concepts. The contract no_write_attestation diff only shows the 10 new true-valued builds_*/exercises_* keys being added; the new false-valued route-dispatch keys are not shown as added. If those keys were absent the loop would emit 'no_write_attestation.<key> is required' and fail. Operator-reported rp04:master-data:validate passing implies they are present (presumably already carried from CP164), but their presence cannot be confirmed from the provided diff alone.
findings.recommendation: No change required if rp04:master-data:validate passes as reported; for evidence completeness, confirm contract.no_write_attestation explicitly carries dispatches_review_route:false and dispatches_approval_route:false so the no-write attestation surface is fully self-documenting rather than inherited implicitly.
findings.post_review_disposition: addressed: contract.no_write_attestation explicitly contains dispatches_review_route:false and dispatches_approval_route:false, and rp04:master-data:validate passes the validator loop.
